hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_desc: Talk about your state and the 33 branches with prepositions of place -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- Numbers, Dates & Regions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 4 -- Numbers, Dates & Regions

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
the exact place where something or someone is
b
a large area with its own government, like Sao Paulo
c
a big road that connects cities
d
a local office of a bigger organization
e
the main city of a state or country
f
how far one place is from another
g
a drawing that shows where places are
h
a word or sign that shows how many
i
the line where one area ends and another begins
j
a certain day, month, and year

paras: Flavia works at Sebrae in the state of Sao Paulo. Sao Paulo is a big state in the southeast of Brazil. The capital city is also called Sao Paulo, and it has more than twelve million people. On the wall of Flavia's office, there is a large map with a small red point for every branch.

Sebrae-SP has thirty-three branches in the state. Each branch is in a different city. One branch is in Campinas, about one hundred kilometers from the capital. Another branch is in Ribeirao Preto, in the north of the state, near the border with Minas Gerais. Flavia often drives on the highway to these branches for meetings.

To talk about place, we use in, at, and on. We use in for cities, states, and countries: 'in Sao Paulo,' 'in Brazil.' We use at for a specific point or building: 'at the branch,' 'at the airport.' We use on for a surface or a street: 'on the map,' 'on Avenida Paulista.' Dates matter too: the annual meeting is on March 15th, and the report is due on the first of April.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about a state and its regions

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Flavia lives in Minas Gerais and never travels for work.
Flavia's work covers a big state with thirty-three branches, and we use in, at, and on to talk about places.
The text explains how to draw a map of Brazil.

items: Sebrae-SP has thirty-three branches in the state.
The text says: 'Sebrae-SP has thirty-three branches in the state.'
The capital of Sao Paulo is a small city.
The text says the capital 'has more than twelve million people.'
The branch in Ribeirao Preto is near the border with Minas Gerais.
The text says: 'in the north of the state, near the border with Minas Gerais.'
We use 'at' for cities, states, and countries.
The text says we use 'in' for cities and states, and 'at' for a specific point or building.

parts: the state of Sao Paulo. The map is
the wall of her office. She often has meetings
the branch in Campinas. Ribeirao Preto is
the north of the state, and the annual meeting is

gapfill.text: Remember: in = cities, states, countries (in Brazil). at = a specific point or building (at the office). on = a surface, a street, or a date (on the map, on Avenida Paulista, on March 15th).

bank.label: Useful sentences to talk about your state and your branches
items: Our head office is in Sao Paulo, the capital of the state.
Sebrae-SP has thirty-three branches in the state.
The branch in Campinas is about one hundred kilometers from the capital.
There is a big map on the wall of my office.
I often have meetings at the branch in the north of the state.
Ribeirao Preto is near the border with Minas Gerais.
The annual meeting is on March 15th.
The report is due on the first of April.

lf.text: Use in for cities and states, at for a specific point or building, and on for a surface, a street, or a date.

items.situation: A partner asks where your head office is. Answer with 'in'.
tips: Our head office is in Sao Paulo.
It is in the capital of the state.

items.situation: Say how many branches Sebrae-SP has in the state.
tips: There are thirty-three branches in the state.

items.situation: Describe where the map is in your office.
tips: There is a big map on the wall.
The map is on the wall of my office.

items.situation: Tell a colleague the date of the annual meeting.
tips: The annual meeting is on March 15th.
It is on the fifteenth of March.

items: Describe the map in your office in three sentences: the state, the number of branches, and where the map is. Use your notes.
Say it again, and add two branches with in and at: 'One branch is in Campinas' and 'I have meetings at the branch in the north.'
Now do it with no notes. Sound natural, not memorized.
A partner asks: 'When is the annual meeting?' Answer: 'It is on March 15th, here in Sao Paulo.'

answerkey.title: Reveal the model description
list: Model: 'I work at Sebrae in the state of Sao Paulo. There is a big map on the wall of my office. Sebrae-SP has thirty-three branches in the state. One branch is in Campinas, and another is in Ribeirao Preto, near the border with Minas Gerais. The annual meeting is on March 15th.'
in = cities, states, countries: in Sao Paulo, in Brazil.
at = a specific point or building: at the branch, at the office.
on = a surface, a street, or a date: on the map, on Avenida Paulista, on March 15th.
answerkey.note: in for big areas, at for a point, on for a surface, a street, or a date.

listenings.text: Hi Flavia, this is Bruno from the regional team. Here is the plan for our visits this month. On Monday, we go to the branch in Campinas, about one hundred kilometers from the capital. On Wednesday, we drive on the highway to Ribeirao Preto, in the north of the state, near the border with Minas Gerais. The big meeting is on March 15th, here in Sao Paulo. Please put these dates on your schedule. Thank you, and have a good trip.

Good morning, everyone. This is a short reminder about our map project. On the wall of the main office, there is a new map of the state with all thirty-three branches. Please check the location of your branch and the distance to the capital. The report with the numbers and the dates is due on the first of April. Remember, every branch is important, from the capital to the border. Thank you, and have a great week.

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia looks at the map on the wall of her office. She checks the location of each branch in the state. On Monday, she visits the branch in Campinas. On Wednesday, she drives on the highway to the north. The big meeting is on March 15th, in the capital.
